matches.x: A™ the Lord spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they were come out of the land

“The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the Tent of Meeting, on the first of the second month, in the second year of their exodus from the land of Egypt, saying” (Numbers 1:1). “The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai” – that is what the verse said: “[Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains]; Your justice is like the great depths” (Psalms 36:7).1“Great depths”…
A™ the Lord spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they were come out of the land

AND Josiah kept a passover unto the Lord in 1 Jerusalem: and they killed the passover on the fourteenth day of the first month. And he set 2 the priests in their charges, and encouraged them to the service of the house of the Lord.

but they did eat of the unleavened bread among their brethren.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: The Torah; A Women's Commentary, Exodus 12:15:1
matches.x: you shall eat unleavened bread. The exact significance here of matzah is unclear. The Bible presents unleavened bread as both a festive food (Genesis 19:3) and a symbol of oppression, associating “the bread of distress” with the memory of the Exodus (Deuteronomy 16:3).
but they did eat of the unleavened bread among their brethren.
